attributes.stem:
A 28-year-old nulliparous woman presents to her general practitioner with a one month history of an intermittent thick and white discharge from her nipples. Her last menstrual period was eight weeks ago and prior to that she had a regular 28-day interval cycle with five days of menstrual bleeding. She has not noticed any abnormal vaginal bleeding, spotting, or discharge. She is sexually active with one partner and she is up to date with her cervical screening with no abnormal results. Her history includes schizophrenia, for which she was started on risperidone six weeks ago, chronic back pain, treated with paracetamol and insulin-dependent type 1 diabetes mellitus.

On examination, she appears calm and comfortable. On breast examination, there is no breast tenderness or masses felt. Gentle compression produces a milk-like nipple discharge from each nipple. Pelvic and abdominal examination is unremarkable. Her b-HCG test is negative.
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the following is the most likely cause of her nipple discharge?
attributes.explanation:
This patient presents with a milky discharge, which is most likely the result of hyperprolactinaemia. She is not pregnant.

Risperidone is an atypical second-generation anti-psychotic medication. Its mechanism of action is through antagonism of dopamine 2 receptors. A well recognised, but uncommon, side effect of risperidone is hyperprolactinaemia. This is due to the downregulation of dopamine which results in less inhibitory signals in prolactin secretion. Hyperprolactinaemia may result in galactorrhoea, gynaecomastia, amenorrhoea, or infertility. When galactorrhoea does occur, the median time of onset after starting the drug is 20 days.

Prolactinoma is a type of pituitary adenoma that is capable of secreting prolactin. It is the most common type of pituitary adenoma. It is a possible diagnosis as it can explain her symptoms. However, it is usually accompanied by other symptoms such as visual changes (bitemporal hemianopia due to optic chiasm compression). Although it is a possible diagnosis, it is not the most likely diagnosis.

Cushing's disease refers to a state of hypercortisolism caused by excessive ACTH from a pituitary adenoma. Hypercortisolism does not cause nipple discharge or increased levels of prolactin; in fact, prolactin increases the level of cortisol, whereas cortisol reduces prolactin release.

Increased levels of prolactin are seen in late pregnancy, mostly during the third trimester onwards. This is a physiological response to prepare the mother for breastfeeding. However, since this patient has a negative b-HCG test, this makes it highly unlikely. Furthermore, increased prolactin levels are unlikely to happen during the early stages of pregnancy.

Mammary duct ectasia is a benign non-proliferative breast disease that is often associated with dilated lactiferous ducts, periductal inflammation and fibrosis. Common complaints include nipple discharge (of various colour), pain and a lump around the areolar region.
learningPoints: Risperidone can be associated with nipple discharge.
references: Mullen J, Jibson MD, Sweitzer D. A comparison of the relative safety, efficacy, and tolerability of quetiapine and risperidone in outpatients with schizophrenia and other psychotic disorders: the quetiapine experience with safety and tolerability (QUEST) study. Clin Ther 2001; 23:1839.
Seeman P. Atypical antipsychotics: mechanism of action. Can J Psychiatry 2002; 47:27.

Hamwi MW, Winters R. Mammary Duct Ectasia. [Updated 2023 Jan 3]. In: StatPearls [Internet]. Treasure Island (FL): StatPearls Publishing; 2023 Jan-. Available from: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK557665/

Borski RJ, Helms LM, Richman NH 3rd, Grau EG. Cortisol rapidly reduces prolactin release and cAMP and 45Ca2+ accumulation in the cichlid fish pituitary in vitro. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A. 1991 Apr 1;88(7):2758-62. doi: 10.1073/pnas.88.7.2758. PMID: 11607172; PMCID: PMC51318.

Russ S, Anastasopoulou C, Shafiq I. Pituitary Adenoma. [Updated 2023 Mar 27]. In: StatPearls [Internet]. Treasure Island (FL): StatPearls Publishing; 2023 Jan-. Available from: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK554451/
